Build user message with intent spec and fetched context\n        user_message = self._build_user_message(request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Synthesizing intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.5,  # Lower temperature for consistent output\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            result_data = json.loads(raw)\n\n            # Extract fields\n            data = result_data.get(\"data\", {})\n            summary = result_data.get(\"summary\", \"\")\n            urgency_str = result_data.get(\"urgency\", request.urgency)\n\n            # Map urgency string to enum\n            try:\n                urgency = Urgency(urgency_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                urgency = Urgency.NORMAL\n\n            # Build coverage and caveats from fetch result\n            coverage = None\n            caveats = None\n            if request.fetched_context:\n                coverage = {\n                    \"total_sources\": request.fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                    \"succeeded\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                    \"timed_out\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                    \"failed\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n                }\n                caveats = request.fetched_context.caveats\n\n            result = SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=urgency,\n                coverage=coverage,\n                caveats=caveats,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Synthesis complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(data)} data fields, urgency={urgency.value}\"\n            )\n\n            return result\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return minimal result\n            return SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data={\"type\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to parse synthesis response\"},\n                summary=\"An error occurred while processing the result.\",\n                urgency=Urgency.NORMAL,\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Synthesis failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_user_message(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build user message for LLM call.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"## Intent Specification\",\n            f\"Intent Type: {request.intent_type.value}\",\n            f\"Project: {request.project_slug or 'none'}\",\n            f\"Urgency: {request.urgency}\",\n            f\"\",\n            f\"## Utterance\",\n            f\"{request.utterance}\",\n            f\"\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.fetched_context:\n            lines.append(\"## Fetched Context\")\n\n            # Add results from each source\n            for source, source_result in request.fetched_context.sources.items():\n                lines.append(f\"\\n### {source.value}\")\n                if source_result.status == \"success\":\n                    # Pretty-print the data\n                    lines.append(f\"```json\")\n                    lines.append(json.dumps(source_result.data, indent=2))\n                    lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # Add coverage info\n            if request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                lines.append(f\"\\n## Caveats\")\n                for caveat in request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                    lines.append(f\"- {caveat}\")\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\nPlease synthesize this into a structured result.\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# Global synthesize strand instance\n_synthesize_strand: Optional[SynthesizeStrand] = None\n\n\ndef get_synthesize_strand(prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> SynthesizeStrand:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global synthesize strand instance.\"\"\"\n    global _synthesize_strand\n    if _synthesize_strand is None:\n        _synthesize_strand = SynthesizeStrand(prompt_path=prompt_path)\n    return _synthesize_strand\n\n\nasync def synthesize_intent(request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to synthesize an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: SynthesizeRequest\n\n    Returns:\n        SynthesizeResult\n    \"\"\"\n    strand = get_synthesize_strand()\n    return await strand.synthesize(request)\n","numLines":267,"startLine":1,"totalLines":267}}}

Synthesize First Token Not Captured\n244\t**Issue:** `synthesize_first_token_ms` shows `count: 0` across all 206 records.\n245\t\n246\t**Impact:** Cannot validate the ~1s first-token budget — this is the binding gate per the plan's internal-consistency note.\n247\t\n248\t**Diagnosis:** Streaming token timing logic in the server is not functioning.   message = \"Fetch config validation failed:\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(f\"  - {e}\" for e in errors)\n83\t        super().__init__(message)\n84\t\n85\t\n86\tdef _validate_timeout_ms(value: Any, source_name: str) -> int | None:\n87\t    \"\"\"\n88\t    Validate a timeout_ms value.\n89\t\n90\t    Args:\n91\t        value: The timeout value to validate\n92\t        source_name: Name of the source for error messages\n93\t\n94\t    Returns:\n95\t        The validated timeout as int in seconds, or None if not specified\n96\t\n97\t    Raises:\n98\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If the timeout value is invalid\n99\t    \"\"\"\n100\t    if value is None:\n101\t        return None\n102\t\n103\t    # Must be an integer or float that can be converted to int\n104\t    if not isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n105\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError([\n106\t            f\"{source_name}: timeout_ms must be a number, got {type(value).__name__}\"\n107\t        ])\n108\t\n109\t    # Convert to seconds (timeout_ms is in milliseconds)\n110\t    timeout_seconds = int(value) / 1000\n111\t\n112\t    # Must be positive\n113\t    if timeout_seconds <= 0:\n114\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError([\n115\t            f\"{source_name}: timeout_ms must be positive, got {value}\"\n116\t        ])\n117\t\n118\t    return timeout_seconds\n119\t\n120\t\n121\tdef _load_fetch_config() -> dict:\n122\t    \"\"\"\n123\t    Load fetch configuration from YAML file.\n124\t\n125\t    Returns:\n126\t        Dictionary with source timeouts. Empty dict if file not found or invalid.\n127\t\n128\t    Raises:\n129\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If config schema is invalid\n130\t    \"\"\"\n131\t    global _fetch_config_cache, _fetch_config_mtime\n132\t\n133\t    # Check if we need to reload (file modified or not loaded yet)\n134\t    try:\n135\t        current_mtime = FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n136\t        if _fetch_config_cache is not None and current_mtime == _fetch_config_mtime:\n137\t            return _fetch_config_cache\n138\t    except OSError:\n139\t        # File doesn't exist or can't be accessed\n140\t        return {}\n141\t\n142\t    # Load the YAML file\n143\t    try:\n144\t        config = yaml.safe_load(FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.read_text()) or {}\n145\t    except OSError:\n146\t        return {}  # File doesn't exist\n147\t\n148\t    # Validate the config structure\n149\t    errors = []\n150\t\n151\t    if \"sources\" in config:\n152\t        if not isinstance(config[\"sources\"], dict):\n153\t            errors.append(\"'sources' must be a dictionary\")\n154\t        else:\n155\t            # Validate each source timeout\n156\t            for source_name, timeout_config in config[\"sources\"].items():\n157\t                if not isinstance(timeout_config, dict):\n158\t                    errors.append(f\"sources.{source_name}: must be a dictionary, got {type(timeout_config).__name__}\")\n159\t                    continue\n160\t\n161\t                if \"timeout_ms\" in timeout_config:\n162\t                    try:\n163\t                        _validate_timeout_ms(timeout_config[\"timeout_ms\"], f\"sources.{source_name}\")\n164\t                    except FetchConfigValidationError as e:\n165\t                        errors.extend(e.errors)\n166\t\n167\t    if errors:\n168\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError(errors)\n169\t\n170\t    # Cache the validated config\n171\t    _fetch_config_cache = config\n172\t    _fetch_config_mtime = current_mtime\n173\t\n174\t    return config\n175\t\n176\t\n177\tdef get_source_timeout_ms(source: FetchSource, project_slug: str | None = None) -> int | None:\n178\t    \"\"\"\n179\t    Get the configured timeout (in seconds) for a fetch source.\n180\t\n181\t    Checks the config file for timeout_ms value and converts to seconds.\n182\t    Priority order:\n183\t    1. Project-specific override (if project_slug provided)\n184\t    2. Global source-specific timeout\n185\t    3. None (use default from spec)\n186\t\n187\t    Args:\n188\t        source: The fetch source to get timeout for\n189\t        project_slug: Optional project slug for project-specific overrides\n190\t\n191\t    Returns:\n192\t        Timeout in seconds, or None if not configured\n193\t\n194\t    Raises:\n195\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If config is invalid (on hot-reload)\n196\t    \"\"\"\n197\t    try:\n198\t        config = _load_fetch_config()\n199\t    except FetchConfigValidationError:\n200\t        # On validation error, return None (use default) but log would be useful\n201\t        return None\n202\t\n203\t    source_key = source.value  # Use enum value as key\n204\t\n205\t    # Check for project-specific override FIRST (if provided)\n206\t    if project_slug:\n207\t        project_timeouts = config.get(\"project_timeouts\", {})\n208\t        if project_slug in project_timeouts:\n209\t            project_config = project_timeouts[project_slug]\n210\t            if isinstance(project_config, dict) and source_key in project_config:\n211\t                source_override = project_config[source_key]\n212\t                if isinstance(source_override, dict) and \"timeout_ms\" in source_override:\n213\t                    timeout_ms = source_override[\"timeout_ms\"]\n214\t                    if timeout_ms is not None:\n215\t                        return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\n216\t\n217\t    # Check for global source-specific timeout\n218\t    sources_config = config.get(\"sources\", {})\n219\t    if source_key in sources_config:\n220\t        source_config = sources_config[source_key]\n221\t        if \"timeout_ms\" in source_config:\n222\t            timeout_ms = source_config[\"timeout_ms\"]\n223\t            if timeout_ms is not None:\n224\t                # Convert milliseconds to seconds\n225\t                return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\n226\t\n227\t    # No timeout configured\n228\t    return None\n229\t\n230\t\n231\tdef get_effective_timeout(spec: FetchCommandSpec, project_slug: str | None = None) -> int | float:\n232\t    \"\"\"\n233\t    Get the effective timeout for a fetch command spec.\n234\t\n235\t    Priority order:\n236\t    1. Project-specific timeout_ms from config file (if project_slug provided and set)\n237\t    2. Global timeout_ms from config file (if set)\n238\t    3. timeout_seconds from spec (default)\n239\t    4. total_sources: int\n656\t    succeeded: list[FetchSource]\n657\t    timed_out: list[FetchSource]\n658\t    failed: list[FetchSource]\n659\t    skipped: list[FetchSource]\n660\t\n661\t    @property\n662\t    def success_rate(self) -> float:\n663\t        \"\"\"Calculate success rate (0.0 to 1.0).\"\"\"\n664\t        if self.total_sources == 0:\n665\t            return 0.0\n666\t _load_fetch_config() -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load fetch configuration from YAML file.\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with source timeouts. call.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The LLM request\n\n        Returns:\n            LLMResponse with content and usage info\n\n        Raises:\n            LLMTimeoutError: If request times out\n            LLMRateLimitError: If rate limited\n            LLMError: For other errors\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = for rate limit\n            if response.status_code == 429:\n                raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = fill.\n\n        Yields text chunks as they arrive from the API.\n\n        Args:\n            system_prompt: System prompt for the LLM\n            user_message: User message\n            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n            temperature: Sampling temperature\n\n        Yields:\n            Text chunks as they arrive\n\n        Returns:\n            Complete response text and usage info in the final iteration\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = request.to_payload()\n            payload[\"stream\"] = True  # Enable streaming\n\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming request: \"application/json\"},\n            ) as response:\n                # Check for rate limit\n                if response.status_code == 429:\n                    raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n                response.raise_for_status()\n\n                accumulated_text = []\n                input_tokens = 0\n                output_tokens = 0\n\n                # \"message_stop\":\n                                usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n                                input_tokens = usage.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n                                output_tokens = usage.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n\n                        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse streaming event: {e}\")\n\n                # Yield final result with usage info\n                full_text = \"\".join(accumulated_text)\n                logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming complete: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}\")\n                yield {\n                    \"text\": full_text,\n                    \"input_tokens\": input_tokens,\n                    \"output_tokens\": output_tokens,\n                    \"finish_reason\": \"stop\",\n                }\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out after {self.timeout}s\") from e\n        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out: {e}\") from e\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError {e.response.status_code}\") from e\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, LLMRateLimitError, LLMError):\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM streaming Your job is to take fetched context and produce a structured result with a conversational summary.\n\n## Input\n\nYou receive:\n- `intent_spec`: The routed intent (project, intent_type, urgency)\n- `fetched_context`: Raw data from fetch sources (kubectl, git, ArgoCD, beads, etc.)\n\n## Output Format\n\n```json\n{\n  \"data\": {\n    \"type\": \"pod-status|git-log|bead-list|workflow-status|...\",\n    \"items\": [\n      {\n        \"name\": \"item-name\",\n        \"status\": \"status-value\",\n        \"details\": { /* structured data for the component */ }\n      }\n    ],\n    \"summary_fields\": {\n      \"total\": 10,\n      \"healthy\": 8,\n      \"degraded\": 2\n    }\n  },\n  \"summary\": \"2-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode. Include what changed, what's actionable, and any caveats.\",\n  \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\"\n}\n```\n\n## Summary Writing Guidelines\n\n- **Lead with what changed**: \"Pipeline is behind by 8 minutes\"\n- **Include causality**: \"Restart count increased because of OOMKilled\"\n- **Surface actionable items**: \"3 pods need manual intervention\"\n- **Note caveats**: \"ArgoCD API didn't respond, using cached sync state\"\n- **Keep it conversational**: Write as if speaking to the user\n\n## Data Structuring\n\nThe `data` field must be structured for the component library. Common patterns:\n\n### Pod Status\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"pod-status\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"pod-name\",\n      \"namespace\": \"namespace\",\n      \"phase\": \"Running|Pending|Failed|Unknown\",\n      \"restarts\": 3,\n      \"age\": \"2d\",\n      \"image\": \"image:tag\",\n      \"node\": \"node-name\",\n      \"ready\": \"1/1\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"summary_fields\": {\n    \"total\": 5,\n    \"running\": 4,\n    \"pending\": 1,\n    \"total_restarts\": 7\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Git Log\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"git-log\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"hash\": \"abc123\",\n      \"message\": \"commit message\",\n      \"author\": \"author\",\n      \"date\": \"2h ago\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"summary_fields\": {\n    \"total\": 10,\n    \"recent_author\": \"jedarden\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Bead List\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"bead-list\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"bead-id\",\n      \"subject\": \"bead subject\",\n      \"status\": \"pending|in_progress|completed\",\n      \"priority\": \"critical|high|normal|low\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"summary_fields\": {\n    \"total\": 5,\n    \"in_progress\": 2,\n    \"blocked\": 1\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Handling Partial Context\n\nSome fetch sources may fail or timeout. Include what you have:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"data\": { /* from sources that responded */ },\n  \"summary\": \"...\",\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"kubectl_pods\": \"success\",\n    \"argocd_sync\": \"timeout\",\n    \"git_log\": \"success\"\n  },\n  \"caveats\": [\"ArgoCD sync status is cached from 5 minutes ago\"]\n}\n```\n\n## Intent Type Handling\n\n- **status**: Emphasize current state and what changed since last check\n- **action**: Confirm execution and surface any failures\n- **brainstorm**: Structure options as a list with pros/cons\n- **lookup**: Extract the specific information requested, format for readability\n- **reminder**: Include the reminder details and when it's due\n\n## Detail Level\n\nMatch detail level to urgency:\n- **critical**: Include all available fields, error messages, logs\n- **high**: Include relevant details, recent changes\n- **normal**: Standard detail, summary of current state\n- **low**: Minimal detail, just the essentials\n\nThe user needs to understand what's happening and what to do about it. Be concise but complete.\n","numLines":145,"startLine":1,"totalLines":145}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"message_stop"},"session_id":"ceb57f1f-05b5-4774-93f2-842050f1a0a0","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"eaf02ee6-9c69-4828-969b-33de3aabab92"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_717e744817f64b71a3a9a998","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n14\t\n15\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Core Architecture\n20\t\n21\t### The Hot Path\n22\t\n23\t```\n24\tUser utterance (voice or text)\n25\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n26\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms est.)\n27\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment,\n28\t                lookup_kind (lookup intents only)}, ...]\n29\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n30\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n31\t             (lookup intents: intent_type + lookup_kind)\n32\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s est.)\n33\t      output: {data, summary} — urgency carries through unchanged from the\n34\t              intent thread (see Urgency ownership)\n35\t  → Server-side card render (no LLM): deterministic component lookup via\n36\t      component_usage_patterns, template filled into card_cache;\n37\t      no match → result flagged for the built-in fallback card\n38\t  → Cards streamed via SSE to active canvas — client injects the rendered\n39\t      HTML, or renders fallback-flagged results with its built-in generic card\n40\t```\n41\t\n42\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n43\t\n44\t> The ~500ms and ~1-2s figures above — and every per-stage number in this plan — are design **estimates**. Nothing on the hot path has been measured yet. See Latency Budget & Instrumentation.\n45\t\n46\t### Latency Budget & Instrumentation\n47\t\n48\tThe <3s promise is the product's central claim, so it gets a budget, instrumentation, and a gate — not just a diagram annotation. Per-stage targets below are estimates chosen so the end-to-end budget is plausible; the Measured columns show actual captured timings from 206 production-like runs across three test shapes (see bead adc-2xf52 for detailed analysis).\n49\t\n50\t| Stage | Target (ESTIMATE) | Measured p50 | Measured p95 |\n51\t|-------|-------------------|--------------|--------------|\n52\t| STT final transcript (Web Speech API) | ~300ms | *Not measured* (client-side) | *Not measured* (client-side) |\n53\t| Intent Router (haiku-class via ZAI proxy) | ~500ms | **1,587-2,074ms** ❌ | **2,527-4,301ms** ❌ |\n54\t| Fetch — first source returns (surfaces as a per-source progress state on the pending card) | ~500ms | **0-16ms** ✅ | **0-21ms** ✅ |\n55\t| Fetch — window closes (all sources resolved or timed out; gates synthesize start — see Fetch Strand) | ~1s | **0-51ms** ✅ | **0-191ms** ✅ |\n56\t| Synthesize — first token (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy; starts at fetch-window close) | ~1s (cap set by the e2e gate — see internal-consistency note) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) |\n57\t| Synthesize — total (first token through completion) | ~1-2s | **3,108-3,984ms** ❌ | **4,663-7,877ms** ❌ |\n58\t| SSE emit → first card render | ~100ms | *Not measured* | *Not measured* |\n59\t| Escalate — bead formulation + safety validation + `bf create` (haiku-class via ZAI proxy; off the first-card path, see note below) | ~2s | **3,992ms** ❌ | **5,445ms** ❌ |\n60\t| **End-to-end: utterance end → first partial card** | **< 3s** | **5,219-5,571ms** ❌ | **8,853-10,404ms** ❌ |\n61\t\n62\t**Internal consistency — the e2e row is the binding gate.** The hot-path stages are strictly sequential (STT → router → fetch-window close → synthesize first token → SSE emit), so their targets must sum inside the end-to-end budget: 300 + 500 + 1000 + 1000 + 100 ms ≈ 2.9s against the 3s gate, leaving ~100ms of unallocated slack. (The fetch first-source and escalate rows sit off this critical path — the former lands inside the fetch window, the latter is off the first-card path per the note below.) Synthesize-first-token was originally sketched at ~1–2s — the Hot Path diagram's ~1–2s figure survives as the estimate for the *full* synthesize call, first token through completion — but at a 2s first token the stages sum to ~3.9s: every stage could pass its own budget individually while the end-to-end gate fails, a contradiction that would otherwise surface only at rehearsal. Hence the ~1s first-token allocation above. All per-stage targets remain ESTIMATES and are **provisional allocations, not independent pass/fail gates**: once the Measured p50/p95 columns fill, re-derive the stage allocations from measured slack (a stage running under budget donates headroom to one running over) with the e2e row held fixed and overriding — the Gate below then applies against the re-derived allocations. A run where every stage meets its allocation but the e2e row misses is still a failed run.\n63\t\n64\t**Task-profile first card.** The escalate stage does not gate the <3s promise: for a task-profile thread, the ack/pending card (a served built-in template, not a component-library render — see Component Library → Built-in cards) renders as soon as the router resolves the thread (router + SSE emit only) — before bead formulation and `bf create` complete — then updates in place with the bead ref when the escalate stage returns. The escalate row above budgets that in-place update, not the first card; the end-to-end row applies to the ack card.\n65\t\n66\t**Instrumentation requirement.** The server records per-stage timings for every dispatch — `router_ms`, `fetch_first_source_ms`, `fetch_total_ms` (fetch-window close), `synthesize_first_token_ms`, `synthesize_total_ms`, `escalate_ms` (task-profile dispatches: formulation + validation + `bf create`), `sse_emit_ms`, plus client-reported STT and first-render timestamps when available — and persists them to the session store's `dispatch_timings` table (schema in Data Model). This is not optional telemetry: it is the only way the Measured columns get filled, and it doubles as the per-step timing log the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist requires.\n67\t\n68\t**Gate.** The demo cannot be scheduled until the Measured p50/p95 columns are filled from real runs (rehearsal timing logs count). If measured p95 blows a stage's budget, either the stage gets fixed or the on-screen promise changes — the recording must not showcase a number the system doesn't hit.\n69\t\n70\t### The Async Path\n71\t\n72\t```\n73\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n74\t  → Generated-Bead Safety validation + approval (see Escalate Strand)\n75\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (bf create)\n76\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n77\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n78\t  → Result written to session store results table\n79\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\t> ⚠ The \"or Telegram if no canvas\" leg is NON-FUNCTIONAL today — Telegram delivery has never worked in any deployment (stubbed methods, unreachable bridge URL; see ADR-1). Until ADR-1 lands, a result arriving with no active canvas is persisted to the session store but reaches no surface.\n83\t\n84\tThe diagram above is the happy path only. Beads also get refused, fail repeatedly, or sit unclaimed — and NEEDLE has no built-in circuit breaker: it re-dispatches a failing bead forever. The async path therefore also specifies:\n85\t\n86\t- **Re-dispatch circuit breaker.** NEEDLE exposes no dispatch-attempt API, so the breaker's signal is the bead's own comment stream: a worker that declines a bead appends a structured refusal comment — `bf comment <id> \"REFUSED: <reason>\"` — the same convention the live incident's workers followed when they committed refusal records to the bead. Each tick the watcher checks its open tracked beads (every unresolved `intents.bead_ref`) alongside the closed list, parses comments past a per-bead high-water mark, and persists the running refusal count in the watcher-owned `bead_watch` table (see Data Model) — on disk, so breaker state survives watcher restarts. After N refusals (default 3) or T hours without progress (default 24h — no closure and no new non-refusal comment), it fences the bead — `bf update` to `status=blocked`, so workers stop picking it up — sets the intent's status to `stuck`, and pushes a \"task stuck — needs your input\" card to the active surface with the most recent refusal reason attached.\n87\t- **Terminal failures.** A bead closed as failed/refused resolves its intent to `failed`; the reason surfaces as a card, never silently dropped. User cancellation still maps to `cancelled`.\n88\t- **Visible aging.** Pending cards show elapsed time; a card past its SLA is flagged on canvas before the breaker trips, so a stuck task is never invisible. The SLA is defined, not vibes: built-in per-intent-type defaults (task-profile: 6h; hot-path intents flag at 30s pending, since their budget is 3s) with a per-project `sla_hours` override in the registry entry. Flag ownership is split by path: for bead-backed (task-profile) intents the watcher computes `sla_deadline` at bead creation, stores it in `bead_watch`, and stamps `sla_flagged_at` when the flag fires. Hot-path intents have no bead and no watcher row — their 30s flag is owned by the **canvas client**: a pure client-side timer from the local pending placeholder's creation at submit time (the canvas creates the placeholder before any server response — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path) applies the aged-pending treatment with no server round-trip, deliberately, because a hung or wedged server is exactly the failure this flag must survive (see the matching Degraded-State UX row).\n89\t\n90\t### The Self-Improvement Path\n91\t\n92\t```\n93\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n94\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n95\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML)\n96\t      generates update\n97\t      surfaces diff to user\n98\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n99\t```\n100\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ceb57f1f-05b5-4774-93f2-842050f1a0a0","uuid":"e22bec4a-a39b-41ae-8cd9-9fea27e866db","timestamp":"2026-07-24T01:53:41.015Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n\n## Overview\n\naide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n\naide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### The Hot Path\n\n```\nUser utterance (voice or text)\n  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms est.)\n      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment,\n                lookup_kind (lookup intents only)}, ...]\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n             (lookup intents: intent_type + lookup_kind)\n      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s est.)\n      output: {data, summary} — urgency carries through unchanged from the\n              intent thread (see Urgency ownership)\n  → Server-side card render (no LLM): deterministic component lookup via\n      component_usage_patterns, template filled into card_cache;\n      no match → result flagged for the built-in fallback card\n  → Cards streamed via SSE to active canvas — client injects the rendered\n      HTML, or renders fallback-flagged results with its built-in generic card\n```\n\nTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. The escalate row above budgets that in-place update, not the first card; the end-to-end row applies to the ack card.\n\n**Instrumentation requirement.** The server records per-stage timings for every dispatch — `router_ms`, `fetch_first_source_ms`, `fetch_total_ms` (fetch-window close), `synthesize_first_token_ms`, `synthesize_total_ms`, `escalate_ms` (task-profile dispatches: formulation + validation + `bf create`), `sse_emit_ms`, plus client-reported STT and first-render timestamps when available — and persists them to the session store's `dispatch_timings` table (schema in Data Model). If measured p95 blows a stage's budget, either the stage gets fixed or the on-screen promise changes — the recording must not showcase a number the system doesn't hit.\n\n### The Async Path\n\n```\nTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → Generated-Bead Safety validation + approval (see Escalate Strand)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created (bf create)\n  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n  → Result written to session store results table\n  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n```\n\n> ⚠ The \"or Telegram if no canvas\" leg is NON-FUNCTIONAL today — Telegram delivery has never worked in any deployment (stubbed methods, unreachable bridge URL; see ADR-1). Returns \"\" on failure so synthesis still\n        functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    async def synthesize(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Synthesize fetched context into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            request: SynthesizeRequest with fetched context\n\n        Returns:\n            SynthesizeResult with data, summary, and urgency\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        prompt = self._load_prompt()\n\n        # Splice urgency.md rules into the system prompt (hot-reloadable).\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = f\"{prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n        else:\n            system_prompt = prompt\n\n        # Build user message with intent spec and fetched context\n        user_message = self._build_user_message(request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Synthesizing intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.5,  # Lower temperature for consistent output\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            result_data = json.loads(raw)\n\n            # Extract fields\n            data = result_data.get(\"data\", {})\n            summary = result_data.get(\"summary\", \"\")\n            urgency_str = result_data.get(\"urgency\", request.urgency)\n\n            # Map urgency string to enum\n            try:\n                urgency = Urgency(urgency_str)\n            except ValueError:\n                urgency = Urgency.NORMAL\n\n            # Build coverage and caveats from fetch result\n            coverage = None\n            caveats = None\n            if request.fetched_context:\n                coverage = {\n                    \"total_sources\": request.fetched_context.coverage.total_sources,\n                    \"succeeded\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.succeeded),\n                    \"timed_out\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.timed_out),\n                    \"failed\": len(request.fetched_context.coverage.failed),\n                }\n                caveats = request.fetched_context.caveats\n\n            result = SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=urgency,\n                coverage=coverage,\n                caveats=caveats,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Synthesis complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(data)} data fields, urgency={urgency.value}\"\n            )\n\n            return result\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return minimal result\n            return SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                data={\"type\": \"error\", \"error\": \"Failed to parse synthesis response\"},\n                summary=\"An error occurred while processing the result.\",\n                urgency=Urgency.NORMAL,\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Synthesis failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_user_message(self, request: SynthesizeRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build user message for LLM call.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"## Intent Specification\",\n            f\"Intent Type: {request.intent_type.value}\",\n            f\"Project: {request.project_slug or 'none'}\",\n            f\"Urgency: {request.urgency}\",\n            f\"\",\n            f\"## Utterance\",\n            f\"{request.utterance}\",\n            f\"\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.fetched_context:\n            lines.append(\"## Fetched Context\")\n\n            # Add results from each source\n            for source, source_result in request.fetched_context.sources.items():\n                lines.append(f\"\\n### {source.value}\")\n                if source_result.status == \"success\":\n                    # Pretty-print the data\n                    lines.append(f\"```json\")\n                    lines.append(json.dumps(source_result.data, indent=2))\n                    lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # Add coverage info\n            if request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                lines.append(f\"\\n## Caveats\")\n                for caveat in request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                    lines.append(f\"- {caveat}\")\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\nPlease synthesize this into a structured result.\")\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# Global synthesize strand instance\n_synthesize_strand: Optional[SynthesizeStrand] = None\n\n\ndef get_synthesize_strand(prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> SynthesizeStrand:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global synthesize strand instance.\"\"\"\n    global _synthesize_strand\n    if _synthesize_strand is None:\n        _synthesize_strand = SynthesizeStrand(prompt_path=prompt_path)\n    return _synthesize_strand\n\n\nasync def synthesize_intent(request: SynthesizeRequest) -> SynthesizeResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to synthesize an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: SynthesizeRequest\n\n    Returns:\n        SynthesizeResult\n    \"\"\"\n    strand = get_synthesize_strand()\n    return await strand.synthesize(request)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":135,"oldLines":9,"newStart":135,"newLines":9,"lines":["             response = await client.call_simple(","                 system_prompt=system_prompt,","                 user_message=user_message,","-                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,","-                max_tokens=4096,","-                temperature=0.5,  # Your job is to take fetched context and produce a structured result with a conversational summary.\n\n## Input\n\nYou receive:\n- `intent_spec`: The routed intent (project, intent_type, urgency)\n- `fetched_context`: Raw data from fetch sources (kubectl, git, ArgoCD, beads, etc.)\n\n## Output Format\n\n```json\n{\n  \"data\": {\n    \"type\": \"pod-status|git-log|bead-list|workflow-status|...\",\n    \"items\": [\n      {\n        \"name\": \"item-name\",\n        \"status\": \"status-value\",\n        \"details\": { /* structured data for the component */ }\n      }\n    ],\n    \"summary_fields\": {\n      \"total\": 10,\n      \"healthy\": 8,\n      \"degraded\": 2\n    }\n  },\n  \"summary\": \"2-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode. Include what changed, what's actionable, and any caveats.\",\n  \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\"\n}\n```\n\n## Summary Writing Guidelines\n\n- **Lead with what changed**: \"Pipeline is behind by 8 minutes\"\n- **Include causality**: \"Restart count increased because of OOMKilled\"\n- **Surface actionable items**: \"3 pods need manual intervention\"\n- **Note caveats**: \"ArgoCD API didn't respond, using cached sync state\"\n- **Keep it conversational**: Write as if speaking to the user\n\n## Data Structuring\n\nThe `data` field must be structured for the component library. Common patterns:\n\n### Pod Status\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"pod-status\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"pod-name\",\n      \"namespace\": \"namespace\",\n      \"phase\": \"Running|Pending|Failed|Unknown\",\n      \"restarts\": 3,\n      \"age\": \"2d\",\n      \"image\": \"image:tag\",\n      \"node\": \"node-name\",\n      \"ready\": \"1/1\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"summary_fields\": {\n    \"total\": 5,\n    \"running\": 4,\n    \"pending\": 1,\n    \"total_restarts\": 7\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Git Log\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"git-log\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"hash\": \"abc123\",\n      \"message\": \"commit message\",\n      \"author\": \"author\",\n      \"date\": \"2h ago\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"summary_fields\": {\n    \"total\": 10,\n    \"recent_author\": \"jedarden\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Bead List\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"bead-list\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"bead-id\",\n      \"subject\": \"bead subject\",\n      \"status\": \"pending|in_progress|completed\",\n      \"priority\": \"critical|high|normal|low\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"summary_fields\": {\n    \"total\": 5,\n    \"in_progress\": 2,\n    \"blocked\": 1\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Handling Partial Context\n\nSome fetch sources may fail or timeout. Include what you have:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"data\": { /* from sources that responded */ },\n  \"summary\": \"...\",\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"kubectl_pods\": \"success\",\n    \"argocd_sync\": \"timeout\",\n    \"git_log\": \"success\"\n  },\n  \"caveats\": [\"ArgoCD sync status is cached from 5 minutes ago\"]\n}\n```\n\n## Intent Type Handling\n\n- **status**: Emphasize current state and what changed since last check\n- **action**: Confirm execution and surface any failures\n- **brainstorm**: Structure options as a list with pros/cons\n- **lookup**: Extract the specific information requested, format for readability\n- **reminder**: Include the reminder details and when it's due\n\n## Detail Level\n\nMatch detail level to urgency:\n- **critical**: Include all available fields, error messages, logs\n- **high**: Include relevant details, recent changes\n- **normal**: Standard detail, summary of current state\n- **low**: Minimal detail, just the essentials\n\nThe user needs to understand what's happening and what to do about it. Be concise but complete.\n","userModified":false}}

Model Selection (Biggest Win)\n**Changed**: `ModelClass.SONNET` → `ModelClass.HAIKU`\n**Expected Impact**: 50-70% latency reduction\n- Haiku is significantly faster than Sonnet\n- Sufficient for synthesis tasks (data structuring, not creative generation)\n- Lower cost per invocation\n\n### 2. Token Limit Reduction\n**Changed**: `max_tokens: 4096` → `max_tokens: 1024`\n**Expected Impact**: 20-30% reduction in generation time\n- Most synthesis outputs are < 1024 tokens\n- Prevents unnecessary generation time\n- Fail-fast if output exceeds expected size\n\n### 3. Temperature Optimization\n**Changed**: `temperature: 0.5` → `temperature: 0.3`\n**Expected Impact**: 5-10% reduction in latency\n- Lower temperature = more deterministic outputs\n- Faster convergence on structured data\n- Maintains consistency for synthesis\n\n### 4. Run latency baseline collection again with same test shapes\n2. Compare p50/p95 synthesis times against adc-2xf52 baseline\n3. Verify output quality is maintained with Haiku\n4.
